title: L'imparfait — Describing the past
objective: Describe past situations, habits and how things used to be (A2: can describe past routines and backgrounds).

bridge.title: Bridge from Hindi / English: the imparfait is “used to / was -ing”
bridge.body: The imparfait describes the past — habits, descriptions, background — not single finished events. *Quand j'étais petit, je jouais au cricket* = “When I was little, I used to play cricket.” Form it from the **nous** present stem + endings **-ais, -ais, -ait, -ions, -iez, -aient**. Hindi’s past-habitual (खेलता था / “used to play”) is the closest match — use it as your anchor.

example.french: Pendant mon enfance, j'habitais à Pune.
example.englishGloss: During my childhood, I lived in Pune.

example.french: Mon village était petit et calme.
example.englishGloss: My village was small and quiet.
In the past, we used to play outside.

example.french: Je jouais au cricket chaque dimanche.
example.englishGloss: I used to play cricket every Sunday.

example.french: L'école était près de la maison.
example.englishGloss: The school was near the house.

example.french: Quand j'étais petit, j'aimais les mangues.
example.englishGloss: When I was little, I loved mangoes.

exercises.prompt: Imparfait of « être »: Quand j'___ petit… (when I was little)

exercises.text: Habit in the past: « Autrefois, nous {{}} au cricket. » (jouer)

exercises.prompt: The imparfait is mainly used for…
options: habits and descriptions in the past
exercises.explanation: Imparfait = ongoing/habitual/background past; the passé composé is for single finished actions.

exercises.prompt: Imparfait of « habiter »: Il ___ dans un village. (he used to live)
Match each imparfait form to its subject.
